strengths: Novel claim-level schema with explicit validity intervals and truth-maintenance edges addresses key limitations in existing agent memory systems
Paired set-inclusion gains on abstention and single-session-user slices demonstrate targeted improvements
Efficiency metrics show substantial token reduction vs A-Mem while retaining functionality
weaknesses: Overall accuracy difference vs Flat RAG not statistically significant (p=0.21)
Knowledge-update and temporal-reasoning slices show no improvement
Potential overfitting to specific question types through schema design
required_revisions: Clarify the reported 'overall improvement of -6.7' in conclusion (negative value appears contradictory)
Replace placeholder values like '...' in algorithm description
Provide more detailed analysis of why KU and TR slices remain tied with baseline

strengths: Novel claim-level memory schema with explicit validity intervals and truth-maintenance edges
Paired McNemar tests and bootstrap CIs demonstrate statistically rigorous comparisons
Strong gains on targeted slices (abstention +44.4pp, SSU +9.5pp) with strict set-inclusion dominance
Ablation isolates LLM linker as the causal driver of improvements
Efficiency metrics show 62% token reduction over A-Mem
weaknesses: Overall accuracy (61.3%) not statistically superior to Flat RAG (68.0%)
Regressions on non-targeted slices (SSA, SSP, MS) without router composition
No cross-session user statements handled in current schema
Reliance on LLM linker's lexical gate may miss nuanced contradictions
Oracle router gap (82.0% vs 69.3%) suggests unresolved complementarity issues
required_revisions: Clarify why overall accuracy CI [60.7,75.3] vs [53.3,69.3] is not significant despite 6.7pp drop
Add analysis of cross-session user statements that fail due to session-batched writer
Detail router implementation mechanics (e.g., intent classifier training data)
Discuss limitations of current contradiction/supersede labeling (e.g., temporal resolution)
Include qualitative examples of failed maximum-consistent-subgraph retrievals

strengths: Explicit truth-maintenance mechanisms (validity intervals, contradict/supersede edges) address critical gaps in long-horizon agent memory
Significant gains on abstention and single-session-user slices (44.4pp and 9.5pp improvements) demonstrate targeted effectiveness
Efficiency improvements over A-Mem (-62.1% tokens, -27.4% latency) show practical viability
Hybrid retrieval design preserves flat RAG's strengths while enabling truth-consistent subgraphs for temporal questions
weaknesses: Overall accuracy of standalone TTMG (61.3%) is lower than Flat RAG (68.0%) due to regressions on non-target slices
Reliance on a small LLM linker introduces potential brittleness in contradiction/supersede labeling
Temporal parser's intent classification could benefit from more detailed evaluation
Oracle router's 12.7pp gap suggests limitations in current composition strategy
required_revisions: Clarify router's type-aware decision criteria and its runtime overhead
Provide more details on handling overlapping validity intervals with partial overlaps
Include analysis of false positives/negatives in the LLM linker's edge labeling
Discuss tradeoffs in choosing k_keep=3 and its impact on subgraph consistency

strengths: Novel claim-level memory schema with explicit validity intervals and truth-maintenance edges
Strict paired dominance on abstention (+44.4 pp) and single-session-user (+9.5 pp) slices
Efficient: 62 % fewer tokens than A-Mem, no cross-encoder reranker
Ablation shows LLM linker is causal for all targeted gains
Open-sourced prompts and hyper-parameters
weaknesses: Overall accuracy lower than Flat RAG (61.3 % vs 68.0 %, n.s.) and conclusion mis-states this as an improvement
Very small n (9 abstention, 21 SSU) for headline claims; McNemar p-values non-significant (0.0625–0.50)
Router variant (TTMG+R) only +1.3 pp over Flat RAG, oracle gap 12.7 pp leaves large room for improvement
Evaluation limited to one 150-question stratified slice; no cross-benchmark transfer (LoCoMo, MemoryAgentBench)
No error bars or power analysis for small-n slices; bootstrap CIs overlap for overall accuracy
required_revisions: Correct the conclusion sentence that claims an overall improvement; report the 6.7 pp *decrease*
Add exact 95 % CIs or standard errors for slice-level accuracies and report power given small n
Expand evaluation to full LongMemEval-S (500 q) and at least one additional benchmark
Provide statistical test for the 1.3 pp TTMG+R gain (currently not reported)
Discuss failure modes on SSA/SSP/MS slices and whether writer or linker improvements could mitigate

strengths: Novel claim-level schema with explicit validity intervals and truth-maintenance edges
Strict set-inclusion dominance on abstention (+44 pp) and single-session-user (+9.5 pp) slices
Comprehensive ablation showing the LLM linker is causally necessary for all targeted gains
Lower token cost (-62 %) and latency vs A-Mem while adding truth maintenance
Full prompt templates, hyper-parameters, and seeded stratified split provided for reproducibility
weaknesses: Only 150-question subset evaluated; no power analysis or multiple-comparison correction
McNemar p-values on key claims are > 0.05 (abstention p=0.0625 one-sided, SSU p=0.5)
No statistical test for the –2.6 pp overall drop vs Flat RAG (p=0.21) or for ablation deltas
No baseline that uses identical claim schema without linker, isolating schema contribution
Router relies on ground-truth question-type labels; no realistic intent-classifier error analysis
Failure modes on SSA/SSP/MS slices only qualitatively sketched; no error taxonomy or counts
required_revisions: Run power analysis and correct for multiple comparisons across slices
Report confidence intervals or exact p-values for every ablation delta
Add a ‘claims-only, no-linker’ baseline to separate schema benefit from edge benefit
Evaluate router with a realistic intent classifier (report precision/recall and drop in F1)
Provide per-slice error breakdown and qualitative examples for SSA/SSP/MS regressions
Release code, data splits, and random seeds to let reviewers reproduce exact numbers

strengths: Claim-level schema with validity intervals and provenance is a clean abstraction for truth maintenance
Lightweight LLM linker + lexical gate keeps write-time cost low (-62 % tokens vs A-Mem)
Strict paired dominance on abstention (+44 pp) and single-session-user (+9.5 pp) slices
Router composition (TTMG+R) recovers overall accuracy while keeping truth-maintenance wins
Ablation shows linker is causal: dropping it hurts every targeted slice
Session-batched writer amortises LLM calls; no cross-encoder reranker needed
weaknesses: Only 150-question stratified slice evaluated; statistical power low (e.g. abstention n=9)
Overall accuracy still below Flat RAG (61.3 % vs 68.0 %); regressions on SSA/SSP/MS slices
Oracle upper-bound 82 % shows 12.7 pp gap—complementarity not yet harvested
Hard-edge thresholds (τ_hard=0.7) and bypass values chosen without sensitivity analysis
No throughput numbers under concurrent write load or multi-user scaling
Reader latency 85 s/question is far from interactive; no tail latency reported
required_revisions: Run full LongMemEval-S 500-question set and report per-slice CIs and McNemar p-values
Provide sensitivity curves for τ_hard, τ_sim, k_keep; justify chosen operating point
Measure write throughput: claims/sec with 1,10,100 concurrent sessions and 99 % latency
Include memory cost: graph size (nodes/edges) vs conversation length, and disk footprint
Add failure-case examples where TTMG abstains wrongly or misses updates; quantify rate
Release ingestion+query code and hyper-params to let reviewers reproduce token/latency claims

strengths: Novel application of classical truth maintenance concepts to LLM agent memory
Claim-level schema with validity intervals and explicit contradict/supersede edges is well-motivated
Abstention capability (88.9% vs 44.4%) addresses an important failure mode
Ablation study properly isolates the LLM linker's causal contribution
Efficiency gains over A-Mem baseline (-62.1% tokens) are meaningful
Paired statistical analysis with McNemar tests is appropriate
weaknesses: Key claims rest on very small sample sizes: n=9 for abstention (p=0.0625 one-sided is marginally significant), n=21 for SSU
Overall accuracy REGRESSES: TTMG 61.3% vs Flat RAG 68.0% - the primary system underperforms the baseline
The TTMG+R router solution mostly uses Flat RAG (routes only SSU to TTMG), undermining the claim of TTMG's standalone value
Conclusion contains an error: states 'improvement of -6.7' which contradicts the paper's narrative
Only two baselines compared (Flat RAG, A-Mem); no comparison to MemGPT, MemoryBank, or other recent systems
Single dataset evaluation (LongMemEval-S only); no cross-dataset validation
Large oracle gap (12.7pp between router 69.3% and oracle 82.0%) is not deeply analyzed
required_revisions: Evaluate on the full LongMemEval-S dataset (500 questions) rather than 150-question slice to increase statistical power
Fix the conclusion error: '-6.7 improvement' is confusing and contradicts the abstract
Add comparison to at least one additional recent memory system baseline (e.g., MemGPT)
Report two-sided p-values as primary; one-sided tests require stronger justification
Provide deeper analysis of why TTMG regresses on SSA, SSP, and MS slices
Cross-validate on at least one additional benchmark (LoCoMo or MemoryAgentBench)
Clarify whether the router uses ground-truth question types or predicted types; if ground-truth, this is an upper bound not a deployable system

strengths: Novel problem formulation: truth maintenance for long-horizon agent memory is underexplored
Fair baseline comparison: same embedding model, reader, and retrieval budget across systems
Statistical tests reported: paired McNemar tests and bootstrap CIs attempted
Efficiency analysis included: token counts and latency measured
Ablation study present: linker removal shown to hurt performance
Well-documented: prompt templates and hyperparameters in appendix
weaknesses: Main capability claim (abstention) based on n=9 questions only - severely underpowered
Abstention improvement (p=0.0625 one-sided, p=0.125 two-sided) is NOT statistically significant at α=0.05
SSU improvement (n=21, p=0.50) is not statistically significant
Overall TTMG performance (61.3%) is WORSE than Flat RAG baseline (68.0%)
TTMG+R router routes abstention questions to Flat RAG, losing TTMG's main benefit on that slice
Router uses ground-truth question types - circular evaluation not representative of deployment
Limited ablation coverage: only linker ablation, no schema/component ablations
Conclusion contains factual error: states 'improvement of -6.7' but TTMG regresses overall
No code availability or reproducibility statement
required_revisions: Evaluate on full LongMemEval-S (500 questions) or larger stratified sample to achieve statistical power
Report two-sided p-values as primary; one-sided tests require stronger justification
Fix conclusion error: TTMG regresses overall, does not improve
Implement and evaluate realistic intent classifier for router instead of using ground-truth types
Design TTMG+R routing to preserve abstention benefits (currently routes ABS to Flat RAG)
Add ablations for individual components: claim schema, MCS algorithm, temporal parser, SP gate
Provide systematic failure-mode analysis with error categorization
Include code availability statement and reproducibility checklist

strengths: Well-motivated problem: truth maintenance for long-horizon agents is under-studied
Claim-level schema with validity intervals and contradict/supersede edges is principled
Efficiency gains vs A-Mem are real: 62% token reduction, 27% latency reduction
Ablation properly isolates linker contribution; drops on all slices confirm causal role
Session-batched writer amortizes LLM costs; good systems thinking
Paired McNemar tests and bootstrap CIs show methodological care
weaknesses: Overall accuracy REGRESSES: TTMG 61.3% vs Flat RAG 68.0% (-6.7pp, not an improvement)
Wins are on tiny slices: n=9 abstention, n=21 SSU; statistical power is weak
McNemar p-values mostly insignificant (p=0.0625, 0.21, 0.50, 1.0)
TTMG+R router essentially admits TTMG fails on most slices—routes only SSU to TTMG
Regressions on SSA (-35pp), SSP (-33pp), MS (-7.5pp) are large and unaddressed
85s end-to-end latency is impractical for real-time conversational agents
Oracle gap of 12.7pp (82% vs 69.3%) is large; router is near-trivial
Conclusion misstates results: claims 'improvement of -6.7' which is a regression
required_revisions: Report overall accuracy honestly: TTMG regresses vs Flat RAG, do not frame as improvement
Address regressions on SSA, SSP, MS slices—why does claim schema hurt these?
Provide results on full 500-question LongMemEval-S, not just 150-question slice
Report confidence intervals on per-slice accuracies, not just overall
Clarify deployment story: if router only uses TTMG for 14% of traffic (21/150), what value remains?
Add latency breakdown: what fraction is writer vs linker vs reader?
Discuss failure modes when linker mislabels contradict/supersede edges
